articles.title: Fostering breakthrough AI innovation through customer-back engineering
articles.description: Despite years of digitization, organizations capture less than one-third of the value expected from digital investments, according to McKinsey research. That’s because most big companies begin with technological capabilities and bolt applications onto them, rather than starting with customer needs a
articles.content: Despite years of digitization, organizations capture less than one-third of the value expected from digital investments, according to McKinsey research . That’s because most big companies begin with technological capabilities and bolt applications onto them, rather than starting with customer needs and working backward to technology solutions. Not prioritizing the customer can create fragmented solutions; disjointed customer experiences; and ultimately, failed transformations. Organizations that
articles.author: MIT Technology Review Insights

articles.title: Texas sues Netflix for advertising ‘bait and switch’ and spying
articles.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has filed a lawsuit against Netflix, accusing the company of turning its back on its promise to remain ad-free and safe for kids. The lawsuit, filed on Monday, claims Netflix has "opened Texans' data for inspection by the same Big Ad Tech community it once criticize
articles.content: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has filed a lawsuit against Netflix , accusing the company of turning its back on its promise to remain ad-free and safe for kids. The lawsuit, filed on Monday, claims Netflix has "opened Texans' data for inspection by the same Big Ad Tech community it once criticized for exploiting users in this same way." In the lawsuit, Paxton claims Netflix drove up subscriptions by promoting its platform as an "escape from Big Tech surveillance." But that changed when Netfl
articles.author: Emma Roth

articles.content: I Tested Every AI Coding Assistant in 2026 — Here's My Honest Ranking As a developer who's been using AI coding tools since the early days of Copilot, I've spent the last 6 months systematically testing every major AI coding assistant on the market. articles.title: Does AI Behave Like a Toxic Ex?
articles.description: Is AI that toxic ex you just can’t forget about and can’t move on from? For every task you get assigned, do you run back to your ex ( AI ) for help? If someone suddenly asks you to center a div , do you instantly go back to your ex ( AI ) to solve it? Why am I comparing AI with a toxic ex? No, I nev
articles.content: Is AI that toxic ex you just can’t forget about and can’t move on from? For every task you get assigned, do you run back to your ex ( AI ) for help? If someone suddenly asks you to center a div , do you instantly go back to your ex ( AI ) to solve it? Why am I comparing AI with a toxic ex? No, I never had a toxic ex. But AI can sometimes behave like one. Before anyone comes for me, this is not an anti-AI article. I use AI every day, probably more than I should. But while using it, I started noti

articles.title: Apple brings encrypted RCS chats to iPhone
articles.description: Apple now lets you have encrypted RCS conversations with Android users through the Messages app on iOS. As part of iOS 26.5, which was released on Monday, Apple added support for end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging in beta, meaning that Apple and Google can't see your messages while they're sent. Wit
articles.content: Apple now lets you have encrypted RCS conversations with Android users through the Messages app on iOS. As part of iOS 26.5, which was released on Monday, Apple added support for end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging in beta, meaning that Apple and Google can't see your messages while they're sent. With iOS 26.5, you'll see a lock icon and a small "Encrypted" message at the top of the chat to note when you're having an encrypted conversation with an Android user and are on a carrier that supports t
articles.author: Jay Peters

articles.title: How to Test MCP Servers Before They Break Your CI
articles.description: Most teams install an MCP server and hope it works. That is how you get 3 AM pages. An MCP server is a bridge between AI agents and your tools. It can crash, leak data, or silently return garbage. If your AI agent relies on it, your whole pipeline breaks. MCP means Model Context Protocol (standard t
articles.content: Most teams install an MCP server and hope it works. That is how you get 3 AM pages. An MCP server is a bridge between AI agents and your tools. It can crash, leak data, or silently return garbage. If your AI agent relies on it, your whole pipeline breaks. MCP means Model Context Protocol (standard tool link). Do not only test startup. Test behavior and permissions too. This post is the checklist I run on every MCP server before it touches production. The three-layer test stack Layer What it catc
articles.author: Anton Gulin

articles.title: Palantir’s true believers are wearing this jacket

articles.content: In late April, Palantir - the software company that, in recent years, has perhaps become best known for its defense industry contracts and work with Immigration and Customs Enforcement - announced that it would be adding new products to its merch store. The latest offering was a cotton chore coat. At $239 and in bright blue and black options, the jacket looks like a standard offering that has, by way of photographer Bill Cunningham , trickled down into mainstream menswear for years. This jacket

articles.description: Signature-based detection has always known what it was looking for. Machine learning and autonomous agents are changing the question entirely, shifting from "does this match a known pattern?" to "does this actually make sense in context?"

articles.title: Three things in AI to watch, according to a Nobel-winning economist
articles.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. A few months before he was awarded the Nobel Prize in economics in 2024, Daron Acemoglu published a paper that earned him few fans in Silicon Valley. Contrary to
articles.content: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here . A few months before he was awarded the Nobel Prize in economics in 2024, Daron Acemoglu published a paper that earned him few fans in Silicon Valley. Contrary to what Big Tech CEOs had been promising—an overhaul of all white-collar work—Acemoglu estimated that AI would give only a small boost to US productivity and would not obviate the need for human work. I
articles.author: James O'Donnell

articles.title: Innovation abounds in device charging
articles.description: The changes may be less perceptible than in smartphones, tablets, or wearables, but chargers have also been quietly reinvented over the last decade. At one time a bulky mix of tangled cables and connectors, slow to perform and prone to overheating, they’re now smaller, safer, and faster, thanks to a
articles.content: The changes may be less perceptible than in smartphones, tablets, or wearables, but chargers have also been quietly reinvented over the last decade. At one time a bulky mix of tangled cables and connectors, slow to perform and prone to overheating, they’re now smaller, safer, and faster, thanks to a slew of technological advances. These advances include a switch to gallium nitride (GaN), which has now usurped silicon as the preferred semiconductor, capable of handling higher voltages, faster swi
articles.author: MIT Technology Review Insights

articles.description: Probably the last big updates we'll see before the next versions appear at WWDC.
articles.content: Apple has released version 26.5 of all of its operating systems today: iOS 26.5 , iPadOS 26.5 , macOS 26.5 , watchOS 26.5 , tvOS 26.5 , visionOS 26.5 , and version 26.5 of the HomePod software (whew). None of these are particularly momentous updates, which is pretty normal this late in their lifecycle, but they add a small batch of new features alongside the pile of patches outlined on Apple’s security vulnerabilities page. This is Apple’s first release to support end-to-end encryption for the R
articles.author: Andrew Cunningham

articles.description: For the first time, Google says it has spotted and stopped a zero-day exploit developed with AI. According to a report from Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG), "prominent cyber crime threat actors" were planning to use the vulnerability for a "mass exploitation event" that would have allowed th
articles.content: For the first time, Google says it has spotted and stopped a zero-day exploit developed with AI. According to a report from Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG), "prominent cyber crime threat actors" were planning to use the vulnerability for a "mass exploitation event" that would have allowed them to bypass two-factor authentication on an unnamed "open-source, web-based system administration tool." Google's researchers found hints in the Python script used for the exploit that indicated help
articles.author: Stevie Bonifield

articles.title: GitHub for Beginners: Getting started with OSS contributions
articles.description: Learn how to find opportunities to contribute to the open source community. The post GitHub for Beginners: Getting started with OSS contributions appeared first on The GitHub Blog .
articles.content: Welcome back to GitHub for Beginners. So far, we’ve discussed GitHub Issues and Projects , GitHub Actions , security , GitHub Pages , and Markdown . This time we’re going to talk about open source software and how to contribute to that community. By the end of this post, you’ll know what open source is, how to find projects to work on, how to read an open source repository, as well as start making your first contributions. So let’s get started! As always, if you prefer to
articles.author: Kedasha Kerr

articles.title: Pirates are already playing Forza Horizon 6 days before its launch
articles.description: Crackers take advantage of unencrypted files that briefly appeared on Steam.
articles.content: Playable copies of Microsoft's Forza Horizon 6 have appeared on game piracy sites more than a week before the game's official launch, the apparent result of a mistake in uploading the game's files to Steam over the weekend. Since the early days of Steam, players have been able to preload encrypted versions of supported games well ahead of release so the game is ready to play when the encryption key is released on launch day. But early Sunday morning, Microsoft mistakenly uploaded roughly 155 GB
articles.author: Kyle Orland

articles.title: Samsung made a “mockery” of Dua Lipa by putting her picture on TV boxes, lawsuit says
articles.description: Lipa says her picture contributed to Samsung's "enormous revenue."
articles.content: About a year ago, I was in my parents’ living room, where a new TV sat in its box, waiting to be set up. My sister-in-law pointed to a woman on the packaging and said, “Oh, that’s Dua Lipa!” I barely know who she is, so I didn’t think it was unusual for the singer to be featured on the box. But at least one person thinks it's a big deal: Lipa herself. On Friday, Lipa filed a lawsuit against Samsung for using her image on some of its TV boxes, alleging that its use constitutes copyright infringem
articles.author: Scharon Harding

articles.title: F1 set for another engine tweak in 2027, and what's this about V8s?
articles.description: Rather than dig in, the sport is being proactive about fixing the problem.
articles.content: Formula 1's on-track racing might look a bit different in 2026 than it did in 2006 or 1986, but it's reassuring to know that the sport's off-track action remains as engrossing as ever. Right now, that involves F1's stakeholders trying to get out of a corner they painted themselves into with the introduction of new V6 hybrid power units for 2026. We saw the first stab of that in Miami , with small tweaks meant to return some of the spectacle to qualifying, which succeeded. But it seems the sport
